---
title: "Planktivore Annotation — Training"
subtitle: "Diatom quick-start"
author: "Planktivore team"
format:
  pptx:
    reference-doc: ""       # optional: point to a branded .pptx template
  revealjs:                  # also renders as an interactive HTML slideshow
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
---

## Why annotation quality matters

- Planktivore images are classified by a model trained on **human labels**.
- Inconsistent labels → a confused classifier.
- This deck is the fast on-ramp; the full [guide](../_site/index.html) has every class.

## The three diatoms you'll see most

::: {.incremental}
- **Eucampia** — blunt horns, curved chains, big lens-shaped gaps.
- **Pseudo-nitzschia** — needle-like cells in straight *stepped* ribbons.
- **Chaetoceros** — fine setae (bristles) that cross between cells.
:::

## Eucampia

- Two **blunt polar horns** — not thin bristles.
- Chains **curve, twist, or coil**.
- Large lens-shaped apertures between cells.

![](../classes/eucampia/ideal/01.png)

## Pseudo-nitzschia

- Long, narrow, **needle-like** cells.
- Straight colonies with cells **overlapping end-to-end** (stepped).
- Label to **genus** — species needs EM.

![](../classes/pseudo-nitzschia/ideal/01.png)

## Chaetoceros

- Centric cells in a chain, each with paired **setae**.
- Setae are long and thin and often **cross** between neighbours.

![](../classes/chaetoceros/ideal/01.png)

## Telling them apart

| If you see... | It's probably... |
|---|---|
| Blunt horns + curved chain | Eucampia |
| Needle cells overlapping end-to-end | Pseudo-nitzschia |
| Fine bristles crossing between cells | Chaetoceros |

## When in doubt

- Compare against the **ideal** images first, then the **challenging** ones.
- Too degraded to be sure? Use the project's low-quality/unknown label.
- The look-alike table on each class page is your friend.
